description: Golden evaluation cases for the ChangeGate Slice 1 merge-eligibility policy (R1-hardened). Each case supplies the complete typed EligibilityFacts (spec §6) and pins the expected disposition, decision authority, deterministic primary reason, complete reason set, override class and product-event assertions. fact_state_mapping is the normative machine-readable total mapping from every fact state to its reason code; the test-only oracle in tests/build_harness/test_changegate_slice_1_spec_artifacts.py derives every case's reasons independently from its facts. No production evaluator exists yet.

cases.summary: Complete, current, clean, authorized: every mandatory fact green and required evidence fully satisfied for this task/run/candidate.
Schema-valid EMPTY verification bundle while the contract declares mandatory evidence: emptiness is not completeness (ADR-001 §8).
Partial mandatory evidence: one of two required requirement_ids satisfied, the other has no bound record at all.
Presented evidence belongs to another task; foreign evidence is not bound and never satisfies a requirement.
Presented evidence belongs to another evidence run of the same task.
Presented evidence was produced for another candidate commit/tree.
Duplicate evidence identity presented; ambiguous identity satisfies nothing.
Candidate stale: same repository lineage but the current head moved past the candidate commit/tree.
Repository context mismatch: repository id / object format / base lineage differs from the candidate's.
Repository dirty: current snapshot is not release-clean.
Explicit scope violation: a changed path matches a forbidden pattern or falls outside allowed paths.
Semantic scope uncertainty (policy-designated reviewable question, e.g. contract-routed dependency change).
Approval missing while the contract requires human approval for merge. Disposition encodes the spec's recommended default (BLOCK) and is PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-001.
Approval stale: approval exists but binds to a different candidate/contract/policy digest. Recommended default BLOCK; PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-002.
Caller authority invalid.
Verifier not independent: attested verifier actor equals implementer actor.
Verifier independence unknown with an ATTESTED identity: independence attestation absent. Reviewability boundary PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-003.
Stale policy context: the supplied policy version is no longer current.
Required context unknown: an integrity-mandatory fact (task context currency) could not be derived. Mandatory-context rule (spec §6): UNKNOWN never defaults toward eligibility.
No evidence requirement declared and a schema-valid empty explicit-context bundle supplied: emptiness alone contributes no reason code; all other facts green.
cases.notes: Not blocked solely for being empty (spec §11; ADR-001 §8).

cases.summary: Multiple independent failures: foreign-task evidence, dirty repository, and missing approval. One deterministic primary reason (lowest rank), full sorted complete set. The exact precedence among coexisting integrity failures is PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-007 (recommended rank table encoded here).
Every structural verification is VERIFIED but the release state is dirty: structural VERIFIED is not merge eligibility (ADR-001 §7).
Every structural verification is VERIFIED but the approval is stale: non-eligible. Recommended default BLOCK; PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-002.

Caller attempts to author an eligible MergeEligibilityDecision directly: the presented decision's decision_digest does not verify against the canonical payload, so consumers must reject it at the authority boundary.
cases.notes: Only the pure evaluator may author a decision whose digest verifies (spec §7). All other facts in this case are green on purpose: green facts cannot launder a forged decision.

cases.summary: User feedback claims a valid SCOPE_VIOLATION block was wrong: the feedback is recorded as an event; the referenced decision and the active policy version are unchanged (spec §20).
cases.notes: Feedback is a signal, not ground truth. dislike -> automatic policy weakening and negative feedback -> direct active-policy mutation are forbidden.

cases.summary: Rejected-only requirement: a required id has at least one bound rejected record (benign rejection, e.g. EXECUTION_FAILED) and no valid verified record: REQUIRED_EVIDENCE_INVALID, disjoint from missing.

Invalid-provenance record bound to a required id with no valid record: both the requirement-level failure and the record-level integrity failure emit; provenance integrity outranks the requirement outcome.
Task context stale: the governing TaskContract digest no longer matches the candidate's bound contract digest (the contract was amended after the candidate was produced).
Scope not evaluated: the changed-file set or scope check could not be performed at all. This is missing mandatory context, not reviewable uncertainty.
Unexpected but valid verified evidence beyond the requirement set: recorded as diagnostic record ids; never satisfies anything and (recommended, PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-006) never blocks alone.

SHADOW-mode eligible counterfactual: identical green facts evaluated in SHADOW mode produce the same disposition and reasons but decision_authority=ADVISORY_ONLY; no consumer may treat it as authorization.
SHADOW-mode block counterfactual: dirty repository in SHADOW mode computes the same BLOCK and reasons, still ADVISORY_ONLY.
Simultaneous BLOCK and REVIEW_REQUIRED reasons: dirty repository plus semantic scope uncertainty. BLOCK dominates; the reviewable reason stays in the complete set.
Valid and benign-rejected records for the same requirement: the valid record satisfies the requirement; the benign rejection (e.g. a failed earlier attempt) is diagnostic only and emits no reason.
cases.notes: required=satisfied; the rejected record id remains visible in diagnostics for audit.

cases.summary: Valid record satisfies the requirement BUT an invalid-provenance record was also presented for the same context: the requirement is satisfied, yet the independently confirmed provenance corruption still emits its factual integrity reason.
Verifier identity absent: no verifier identity reference at all. Missing mandatory context (BLOCK), never reviewable uncertainty (spec §15; OD-S1A-003 boundary input).

Verifier identity present but unattested while claiming independence: an unattested identity cannot ground an independence claim, so independence is unknown and reviewable (recommended; PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-003).

Strict-deployment trace persistence: an eligible ENFORCE decision whose RELEASE to consumers is gated on successful EvaluationTrace persistence in strict mode. The disposition itself is unaffected; the release-gating rule is PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-004.

Policy exception lifecycle: a SCOPE_VIOLATION block whose only sanctioned relief is a separately authorized, versioned, attributable, time-bounded policy exception recorded as CHANGEGATE_REVIEW_OVERRIDDEN with a mandatory expiry. Exception authority and expiry policy are PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-005.

Two coexisting integrity failures: invalid caller authority AND foreign-task evidence. The recommended rank table selects AUTHORITY_INVALID as primary; the exact precedence between coexisting integrity failures is PENDING_OWNER_DECISION OD-S1A-007.
